questions.prompt: What must the condition inside an if(...) evaluate to?
options: An int
A String
A boolean (true or false)
Any non-null object
questions.explanation: Java's if requires a boolean condition. Unlike some languages, a number or object is not automatically treated as true/false.

questions.prompt: You need to repeat an action until a loading spinner disappears, with no known number of repeats. Which loop fits best?
options: A for loop counting from 0 to 10
A while loop that runs while the spinner is displayed
An if/else statement
A break statement
questions.explanation: A while loop repeats until a condition flips, which suits polling. A for loop is better when the iteration count is known.

questions.prompt: Inside a loop, what is the difference between break and continue?
options: break skips the current iteration; continue exits the loop
break exits the loop entirely; continue skips to the next iteration
Both exit the loop
Both skip only the current iteration
questions.explanation: break leaves the loop completely; continue abandons the rest of the current pass and moves on to the next iteration.

questions.prompt: When is the expression (isLoggedIn && hasItems) true?
options: When either isLoggedIn or hasItems is true
Only when both isLoggedIn and hasItems are true
When neither is true
Always — && ignores the values
questions.explanation: Logical AND (&&) is true only when both operands are true. The OR operator (||) is the one that is true when either side is true.

questions.prompt: In `for (int i = 0; i < rows.size(); i++)`, what does the middle part do?
options: Initializes the counter once before the loop
Is the condition checked before each iteration; the loop runs while it is true
Increments the counter after each iteration
Exits the loop immediately
questions.explanation: The three for parts are init / condition / step. The middle part is the boolean condition tested before each pass; i++ (the step) is the third part.
